title: Recover Max Heap After Element Removal

statement.markdown: A max heap is a complete binary tree where each node is greater than its children. Given an array representing a max heap and the index of an element to be removed, restore the max heap. The array is 0-indexed, assuming the index of the root node is 0.
statement.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n representing the number of elements in the array. The next line contains n space-separated integers representing the elements in the array. The last input is the index of the element to be removed.
statement.outputFormat: The output contains the restored max heap, each element on a separate line.
statement.notes: When an element is removed, the max heap property must be maintained. You can use any algorithm to restore the heap.

constraints.description: Index of element to be removed

examples.explanation: Restore the max heap after removing the element at index 2 (5 in the array).
Restore the max heap after removing the element at index 0 (7 in the array).
